Robot Inspiration

Examples of completed AgRobotics builds to spark your imagination. Use these as a starting point or design something entirely your own.

Inspiration
Ag Robot 6
Complete AgRobotics robot with Control Hub, servo-driven arm, and line sensor mount. Shows how to extend a chassis with active attachments.
Inspiration
Ag Robot 2
Complete AgRobotics robot with Control Hub and extendable servo arm. Demonstrates beam-based arm construction and servo attachment mounting.
Inspiration
Ag Robot 3
Complete AgRobotics robot with Control Hub, servo arm with gusset joints, and caster wheel. A versatile build showing multi-servo attachment design.
📦

Kit Parts

Familiarize yourself with the parts in your AgRobotics kit. Knowing what each part is called makes it easier to follow instructions and ask for help.

🛠

Assembly Examples

Step-by-step visual guides for common assembly tasks. Follow along to build key sub-assemblies correctly before adding them to your robot.

Assembly
Motor & Wheel Assembly
Attach the motor bracket to the channel first, then slide the motor into the bracket from the front. Secure with the motor retaining screws. Slide the wheel onto the output shaft until it seats fully, then secure the shaft clip.
📸 Photo Coming Soon
Assembly
Mounting the Control Hub
Mount the AgRobotics Control Hub near the center of the chassis to keep the robot balanced. Leave enough clearance around the USB port and motor connectors for easy access.
⚡

Electronics

The AgRobotics Control Hub is the brain of your robot. Learn how to connect motors, power the hub, and use its built-in features.

⚠️ Important: Always power off the controller before connecting or disconnecting motors and sensors. Hot-plugging can damage ports.
Electronics
AgRobotics Control Hub
Features 4 motor ports (M1–M4), 4 servo ports, sensor expansion headers, built-in buzzer, NeoPixel LEDs, USB-C for programming, and Bluetooth for wireless control. The transparent lid lets you see indicator LEDs without opening the enclosure.
📸 Photo Coming Soon
Electronics
Motor Port Assignments
Motor ports are numbered 1–4. By default the Robot Config block assigns Left Wheel to port 1 and Right Wheel to port 2. If your robot turns the wrong direction, try swapping the port numbers before reversing physical wires.
📸 Photo Coming Soon
Electronics
Charging & Battery Care
Use only the supplied LiPo charger. Never leave a LiPo battery charging unattended. Store batteries at partial charge when not in use. A puffed or swollen battery should be taken out of service immediately.
📡

Sensors

Sensors give your robot the ability to perceive its environment. Learn how to mount and wire each sensor type for best results.

📡

Sensor guides coming soon

Coverage for ultrasonic distance, time-of-flight, line following, color, and temperature/humidity sensors will appear here.
